Off Calendar Oct-29-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Erin K. Hayner, Esq. of HAWKINS PARNELL THACKSTON & YOUNG (213-486-8021) appearing for defendant / moving party Sherwin-Williams Co.
Stephen J. Healy, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party George McMillian

RULING:
DEFENDANT SHERWIN-WILLIAMS CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant The Sherwin-Williams Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. By not presenting responses to Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. The 9/21/10 order regarding application of Texas law neither states nor suggests that Texas summary judgment rules apply. (See 3 Witkin, California Procedure (5th ed. 2008 and 2010 supp.) 117 ("The basic rule is well settled that matters of procedure are governed by the law of the forum.")). By not presenting responses to contention discovery with its moving papers, defendant cannot satisfy the factually-devoid prong of Aguilar. Evidentiary materials submitted with reply papers were not considered. Defendant is granted leave to file another motion for summary judgment, if it wishes to do so, that includes responses to contention discovery with its moving papers. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
